name: Chronos (Amazon)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); Amazon Research; models on HuggingFace Hub; no API costs

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace for model downloads (no auth needed)

performance.notes: GPU inference 100-500ms per batch; CPU feasible for small models (Chronos-T5-tiny/small)

agent_readiness.notes: Amazon's pretrained probabilistic time series foundation model. T5-based language model trained on large time series corpus. Zero-shot forecasting — no fine-tuning required. Multiple model sizes (tiny to large). Probabilistic predictions with quantiles. Self-hosted on HuggingFace. Competitive with domain-specific models on M4/M5 benchmarks. Open alternative to proprietary TimeGPT.

gotchas: Larger models require GPU for practical inference
Less performant than TimeGPT on some benchmarks
No built-in anomaly detection or fine-tuning API
Documentation less comprehensive than mature libraries
best_when: Zero-shot time series forecasting with local/private data (no API calls)
Privacy-sensitive forecasting where data cannot leave premises
Research and benchmarking against foundation model approaches
avoid_when: CPU-only environments for large model variants
When fine-tuning on domain data is needed (limited fine-tuning support)
Production scale with many series (proprietary TimeGPT has better SDK)
summary: Chronos is Amazon's open-source pretrained time series foundation model based on T5 transformers. Zero-shot probabilistic forecasting — no fine-tuning required. Multiple model sizes from tiny to large. Runs locally on HuggingFace models. Privacy-preserving alternative to proprietary TimeGPT. Competitive on M4/M5 benchmarks. Best for private zero-shot forecasting without sending data to external APIs.
